name: document
description: Generate or update doc comments / module rustdoc / panel-level READMEs for a target.
prompt_template:
Generate or update documentation for ${target}.

Match the project's existing voice and shape (see ATELIER.md for the conventions). For Rust code, the conventions are:

- Module-level `//!` blocks explain the module's *job* and the invariants it owns — not a line-by-line gloss of its types.
- Public items get one short `///` summary line + a blank line + an optional details block. Skip the comment entirely if the name + signature already say everything.
- Use the `//!` block to cite the spec section the module implements (e.g., `Spec §15 — Skills`) when applicable.
- Example blocks only when the call shape is non-obvious from the signature.

Do not paraphrase the obvious. Document the *why* and the load-bearing invariants — the ones a future contributor would otherwise have to re-derive from the code.

args.name: target
args.description: File path, module path, or symbol name to document.
